coreConcept: The Water Cycle is Earth's amazing way of recycling water! It's how water travels from the ground and oceans up into the sky, forms clouds, and then falls back down again as rain or snow. This endless journey ensures there's always fresh water for plants, animals, and us to use.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Provides the energy to start the water cycle.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun is like the big engine of the water cycle, giving all the energy needed to make water move and change.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Oceans, Lakes, and Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Major sources of water for evaporation and collection points.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: These are the giant bathtubs and flowing paths where most of Earth's water lives and where the water cycle often begins and ends.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Store water vapor and release it as precipitation.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are like fluffy water storage tanks in the sky, holding tiny water droplets or ice crystals until they get too heavy and fall.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Release water vapor into the atmosphere through transpiration.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants are like tiny water pumps, soaking up water from the soil and then breathing some of it out into the air as vapor through their leaves.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Gravity
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Pulls water down from clouds and makes it flow across land.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Gravity is the invisible force that pulls raindrops down from the sky and makes rivers flow downhill, guiding water back to larger bodies.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun heats up water in oceans, lakes, and rivers, turning it into an invisible gas called water vapor that rises into the air.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As the water vapor rises higher, it cools down and changes back into tiny liquid water droplets or ice crystals, forming clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When clouds get full of water droplets or ice crystals, they become heavy, and the water falls back to Earth as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection
storyOfProcess.description: The fallen water gathers in oceans, lakes, and rivers, or soaks into the ground to become groundwater, ready to start the journey all over again.

corePrinciples.name: Water Never Disappears
corePrinciples.description: The total amount of water on Earth stays the same; it just changes form and moves around, always being recycled.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Makes it Move
corePrinciples.description: The Sun's energy is essential for the water cycle, powering the changes from liquid to gas and back again.

corePrinciples.name: It's a Continuous Loop
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle has no real beginning or end; water is always moving and transforming in a never-ending journey.
